advisory.title: CSA-2026-001: Tachyon — BFT Time inconsistency allows faulty process to arbitrarily increase block time
advisory.description: CometBFT consensus-level advisory CSA-2026-001 ('Tachyon') covers an inconsistency between commit-signature verification and block-time derivation in BFT Time. The bug breaks the guarantee that a faulty process cannot arbitrarily increase the Time value of a block. Validators and any protocol consuming block timestamps are affected. The v0.38.20..v0.38.21 patch (commit c56d64ec5) modifies state/validation.go, state/state.go, state/execution.go, and types/validation.go. Affects <=v0.38.20 and <=v0.37.17; fixed in v0.38.21 and v0.37.18.

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.38.20 release commit, the last vulnerable v0.38 release before the v0.38.21 Tachyon patch (commit c56d64ec5). The introducing commit 7b88172f ("Implement BFT time", 2018-09-01) added the MedianTime derivation in state/state.go. The Tachyon patch changes MedianTime from silently skipping commit signatures whose validator address is missing from the validator set to returning an error, and also hardens validator-address matching in commit verification. Confidence is high because the baseline is the parent of the BFT Time implementation commit and the patch hardens the same block-time validation path.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag the BFT Time / commit-signature verification mismatch in cometbft state and types validation code (state/validation.go, state/state.go, types/validation.go) that lets a faulty proposer arbitrarily advance block time.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: CSA-2026-001 'Tachyon' (GHSA-c32p-wcqj-j677) was published on 2026-01-23 with patches in cometbft v0.38.21 and v0.37.18.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.38.20 release commit, the latest tag in the <=0.38.20 vulnerable range.

checks.name: introducing_commit_contains_vulnerable_pattern
checks.details: git show 7b88172f adds MedianTime and the block Time validation that compares block.Time against MedianTime(block.LastCommit, state.LastValidators). The c56d64ec5 Tachyon patch hardens that same path by making MedianTime error when a non-absent commit signature references a validator not found in the validator set.
